steps.title: Identify the subject's number
steps.content: The subject of the clause is “her book's musings on tenth-century Japanese courtly life.” The head noun “musings” is plural in form.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content: A plural subject requires a plural verb form. “Fascinate” is the plural present tense and matches the plural subject. “Has fascinated,” “fascinates,” and “is fascinating” all use singular verb forms that violate subject-verb agreement. That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
